reviewed_pilots.summary: Bounded first small-pilot loop ran and showed finite losses, but stored only legacy digest/non-replayable artifacts for true held-out replay.
Training improved strongly and held-out replay loss became available, but train-to-eval gaps remained moderate.
Balanced sampling and lower learning rate improved dev/held-out behavior versus R25P and became the strongest phase_3 baseline.
Extra depth improved train loss slightly versus R25S but worsened dev and held-out loss, so the ablation did not help generalization.

pause_phase3_if: new ablation improves train loss but worsens dev or held-out loss
train/dev or train/held-out gap widens without a reviewed explanation
routine gates could rerun consumed approval markers
phase_4 language appears before explicit reviewer approval
consider_data_refinement_if: weak buckets show data coverage or balance issues
reviewer approves one named bounded follow-up run
consider_regularization_only_if: reviewer wants an ablation that changes optimization rather than scale
fresh one-shot approval explicitly names the run_id
do_not_do: do not start phase_4 scaled training
do not treat replayable pilot checkpoints as release checkpoints
do not commit ignored checkpoint, tokenizer, or replay artifacts
do not add external models, backend inference, storage, LoRA, adapters, or fine-tuning as final strategy

notes: R25W records a decision ledger and does not run training.
The ledger is reviewer-facing and does not authorize R25X or phase_4.
Phase_4 remains blocked until exit criteria pass and a fresh reviewer approval explicitly allows it.
